raw_response:
**Overall rating: MOSTLY_FALSE.** Git’s `.mailmap` does rewrite author and committer names/emails for Git tools like `git log`, `git blame`, and `git shortlog`, but the claim that GitHub “IGNORES `.mailmap` and reads raw commit metadata” is not supported by current GitHub documentation and is contradicted by GitHub’s own commit-email attribution rules.[1][2][3]

## Short summary
The first half of the statement is true: `.mailmap` is a local Git display-mapping feature. The second half is too absolute and misleading, because GitHub attributes commits by commit email and does not document `.mailmap` as a universal “ignore”/“read raw metadata” rule; GitHub’s behavior is based on email association rather than `.mailmap` processing.[2][3][1]

## Claim analysis

| Claim | Rating | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| `.mailmap` is purely a local display rewrite for `git log`, `git blame`, `git shortlog`. | TRUE | Git’s docs say `.mailmap` is used to map author and committer names/emails to canonical names/emails, and it is specifically intended for Git’s own output tools. [1] |
| GitHub ignores `.mailmap`. | MISLEADING | GitHub docs explain that commit attribution is based on the email address in the commit header and the email addresses added to a GitHub account, not on `.mailmap`; that means GitHub does not rely on `.mailmap` for attribution, but “ignores” is broader than the documentation supports. [2][3] |
| GitHub reads raw commit metadata. | TRUE | GitHub’s documentation states that it uses the email address in the commit header to link commits to a user, which means attribution is derived from commit metadata rather than from `.mailmap`. [2][3] |

## Why this matters
`.mailmap` affects how Git presents history locally, but GitHub’s commit attribution system works differently and is tied to verified email addresses on the account. So the accurate takeaway is that `.mailmap` does not control GitHub profile attribution in the same way it controls Git command output.[3][1][2]

## Sources
- Git `.mailmap` documentation: https://git-scm.com/docs/gitmailmap[1]
- GitHub Docs, setting commit email address: https://docs.github.com/en/account-and-profile/how-tos/email-preferences/setting-your-commit-email-address[2]
- GitHub Enterprise docs, why commits are linked to the wrong user: https://docs.github.com/en/enterprise/2.15/user/articles/why-are-my-commits-linked-to-the-wrong-user[3]
